summary: I live in Chippewa Falls, and I really like it here. It's a great place to live, and I also enjoy the nearby Eau Claire area. Living in Chippewa Falls has been comfortable for me, especially as I navigated working full-time during the pandemic.

I went to the University of Wisconsin–Eau Claire, where I was involved in a sorority and lived in the sorority house with nine other women. Eau Claire was a big part of my college life, filled with classes, internships, and social activities. The pandemic really changed how I experienced Eau Claire, especially during my senior year when everything shut down.

When COVID-19 first started, I wasn't too concerned because I was young, but everything changed quickly when things shut down, including a Minnesota Wild hockey game I was supposed to attend. The pandemic disrupted my senior year at UW-Eau Claire and my job prospects, as my internship offer was rescinded. It also changed how I communicated with friends and family, forcing me into quarantine and isolation, which was mentally challenging. Over time, I got vaccinated and adapted to new safety measures while trying to maintain social connections.

I was supposed to go to a Minnesota Wild hockey game the day everything shut down due to COVID-19, but it got canceled as the pandemic escalated. That moment really marked the sudden shift in how serious the situation was. It was one of the first signs for me that life was about to change drastically.

I graduated from the University of Wisconsin–Eau Claire during the pandemic, which was a tough time because my senior year was cut short and I missed out on closure with my friends. I was active in a sorority and lived in the sorority house, so the transition to isolation was hard. I also had an internship lined up through UW-Eau Claire, but it was rescinded due to COVID-19, which forced me to move back home and rethink my career path.

I received the Pfizer vaccine and found the process very smooth and quick, taking less than 10 minutes. I didn't experience any significant side effects besides a headache that I think was unrelated. Getting vaccinated made me feel better about being around my mom, who has health issues, even though it didn't drastically change my behavior since you can still get COVID after vaccination.

Durand, Wisconsin was mentioned in relation to my boyfriend's family, who had COVID-19 while my immediate family did not. It was concerning because my mom has health issues, and we had recently been at my parents' house in that area. The situation made me very cautious about exposure and quarantine measures.
